nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 徐畢克斯
nodes.summary: 徐畢克斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联14种鸟类，包括白腹拟䳍、黄腿穴䳍、Scarlet-horned manakin等14种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: Rufous-tailed flatbill
nodes.summary: Rufous-tailed flatbill（Rufous-tailed flatbill，学名 Ramphotrigon ruficauda），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括稀树草原，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: 森林
nodes.summary: 森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联402种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan等402种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 圭亚那 (地区)
nodes.summary: 圭亚那 (地区)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联92种鸟类，包括Blue-throated piping guan、Black curassow、纯色秧鸡等92种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Várzea forest
nodes.summary: Várzea forest是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联206种鸟类，包括Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan、Spix's guan等206种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The rufous-tailed flatbill (Ramphotrigon ruficauda) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in every mainland South American country except Argentina, Chile, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: From that area it extends east across southern and eastern Venezuela, the Guianas, and essentially all of Amazonian Brazil. It inhabits humid evergreen forest both várzea and terra firme and also, in Brazil, wooded savanna. In elevation in Brazil it ranges from sea level to . It reaches in Colombia, in Ecuador, in Peru, and in Venezuela.{{cite book | last1 =McMullan | first1 =Miles | last2 =Donegan | first2 =Thomas M

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is considered rare in Colombia, "rare and local" in Ecuador, "uncommon but locally fairly common" in Peru, "uncommon to fairly common" in Venezuela, and fairly common in Brazil. "Human activity has little direct effect on the Rufous-tailed Flatbill, other than the local effects of habitat destruction."
